data.body:
Goal:
Make the Foolery settings schema the authoritative, self-documenting, machine-readable spec so any frontier agent (Claude, Codex, OpenCode, Gemini, Copilot) can read an exact definition of the target file and produce a valid ~/.config/foolery/settings.toml. This is the prerequisite for replacing the ~3000-line `foolery setup` bash wizard with an agent-driven skill.

Verification:
1. `bun run lint` exits 0.
2. `bunx tsc --noEmit` exits 0.
3. `bun run test` exits 0 (all green).
4. `bun run build` exits 0.
5. Run `foolery config schema` (after rebuild via `bash scripts/build-cli.sh` if needed) and pipe to `jq ".properties | keys"`; expect the top-level keys `agents`, `actions`, `backend`, `defaults`, `scopeRefinement`, `pools`, `dispatchMode`, `maxConcurrentSessions`, `maxClaimsPerQueueType`, `terminalLightTheme` to all be present.
6. Pipe the same output to `jq ".properties.agents.description"` and confirm a non-null description string (proof that `.describe(...)` flowed into JSON Schema output).
7. `foolery config validate ~/.config/foolery/settings.toml` prints "OK" to stdout and exits 0 against the current user config.
8. Create `/tmp/foolery-bad.toml` containing `maxConcurrentSessions = 999`, run `foolery config validate /tmp/foolery-bad.toml`; expect exit code 1 and a stderr message naming the field `maxConcurrentSessions` and the valid range (1-20).
9. Open `docs/SETTINGS.md`: confirm every top-level field in `foolerySettingsSchema` is documented with name, type, default, validation range (where applicable), description, and example. Confirm the file states that `src/lib/schemas.ts` is the authoritative source.

Constraints:
- Do NOT remove `scripts/setup.sh` or the wizard scripts; that is a follow-on knot.
- Do NOT mark this knot SHIPPED — the user will review.
- Prefer Zod v4 native `z.toJSONSchema()` over adding new dependencies; only add `zod-to-json-schema` if v4 native is insufficient, and document why in the ship notes.
- Max 500 LOC per file, max 100 LOC per function, max 100 columns per line (project-enforced by ESLint).
- Follow "fail loudly, never silently" — validate errors must name the exact field path and expected shape.
data.title: Self-document Foolery settings schema for agent-driven config
